question: Operators flagged a short window of unusual top-of-winding temperature readings on T-014 over the last few days. First identify the sensor channels installed on this asset, then run an anomaly check and trend analysis on the winding temperature channel (winding_temp_top_c) to determine whether the deviations form a single burst or a sustained shift.
answer:
The sensor channels installed on T-014 are load_current_a, oil_temp_c, power_factor, voltage_hv_kv, voltage_lv_kv, and winding_temp_top_c. 

The detect_anomalies function call on the winding_temp_top_c sensor channel indicates that there are 2 anomalies in the data, which is 0.28% of the total readings. The anomalies occurred on January 16, 2024, at 05:00:00 and January 28, 2024, at 06:00:00, with values of 131.905°C and 116.774°C, respectively.

The trend_analysis function call on the winding_temp_top_c sensor channel shows that the mean value of the sensor readings is 79.6196°C, with a minimum value of 40.231°C and a maximum value of 136.394°C. The slope of the trend is 0.046413°C per day, indicating a stable direction. The R-squared value is 0.0007, indicating a weak correlation between the sensor readings and time. 

These results suggest that the deviations in the top-of-winding temperature readings on T-014 are not part of a sustained shift, but rather a single burst of anomalies.
